revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=阿志都彌神社・行過天満宮| 所在地=滋賀県高島市今津町弘川1707-1| 祭神=木花開耶姫命
菅原道眞公| 社格=式内社
郷社| 創建=創祀年代不詳| 例祭=[[4月25日]]| 神事=}} '''阿志都彌神社・行過天満宮'''（あしづみじんじゃ・ゆきすぎてんまんぐう）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]今津町弘川にある神社である。[[式内社]]の[[論社]]である。旧社格は郷社。 ==ご祭神== *阿志都彌神社 ** [[木花開耶姫命]] *行過天満宮 ** [[菅原道真]]公 ==ご神紋== 山桜 ==歴史== 創祀年代不詳であるが、社伝によると、社殿を持たなかった時代、この地にあった山桜の木に[[木花開耶姫命]]を勧請し、桜花大明神と称した。[[長徳]]4年に[[菅原輔正]]が[[菅原道真]]公を勧請し、行過天満天神を併せ祀ったと伝えられている。阿志都彌神社は、[[式内社]]論社である。善積郷の惣社である。旧郷社。 ==祭事== 例祭　　4月25日 ==境内社== * 稲荷神社 == 関連項目 == *[[神道]] *[[神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:あしつみしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:高島市]] [[Category:式内社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=阿志都彌神社・行過天満宮| 所在地=滋賀県高島市今津町弘川1707-1| 祭神=木花開耶姫命
菅原道眞公| 社格=式内社

{{神社| 名称=阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮| 所在地=滋賀県高島市今津町弘川1707-1| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|24|24|N|136|01|33|E|}}| 祭神=木花開耶姫命
菅原道眞公| 社格=式内社
郷社| 創建=創祀年代不詳| 例祭=[[4月25日]]| 神事=}} '''阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮'''（あしづみじんじゃ・ゆきすぎてんまんぐう）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]今津町弘川にある神社である。[[式内社]]の[[論社]]である。旧社格は郷社。 ==祭神== *阿志都弥神社 ** [[木花開耶姫命]] *行過天満宮 ** [[菅原道真]]公 ==神紋== :山桜 ==歴史== :創祀年代不詳であるが、社伝によると、社殿を持たなかった時代、この地にあった山桜の木に[[木花開耶姫命]]を勧請し、桜花大明神と称した。[[長徳]]4年に[[菅原輔正]]が[[菅原道真]]公を勧請し、行過天満天神を併せ祀ったと伝えられている。阿志都弥神社は、[[式内社]]論社である。善積郷の惣社である。旧郷社。 ==社家== :神職は、田谷家が代々世襲する。 ==祭事== :例祭　　4月25日 ==境内社== * 稲荷神社 * 白山神社　……　北生見・南生見に鎮座していた２つの白山神社を遷座した。 == 関連項目 == * [[神道]] * [[神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:あしつみしんしやゆきすきてんまんくう}} [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:郷社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 阿志都弥神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 滋賀県高島市今津町弘川1707-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|24|24.0|N|136|01|32.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 木花開耶姫命
菅原道真公 |社格 = 式内社・旧郷社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造|三間社流造]] |別名 = 桜花大明神・行過天満宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月25日]] |神事= }} '''阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮'''（あしづみじんじゃ・ゆきすぎてんまんぐう）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]今津町弘川にある神社である。[[式内社]]の[[論社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 ==祭神== *阿志都弥神社 ** [[木花開耶姫命]] *行過天満宮 ** [[菅原道真公]] ==神紋== *山桜 ==歴史== 創祀年代不詳であるが、社伝によると、社殿を持たなかった時代、この地にあった山桜の木に木花開耶姫命を[[勧請]]し、「桜花大明神」と称した。[[長徳]]4年（[[998年]]）に[[菅原輔正]]が菅原道真公を勧請し、行過天満天神を併せ祀ったと伝えられている。阿志都弥神社は、式内社論社である。善積郷の惣社である。旧郷社。 ==祭祀== === 神事 === 例祭は4月25日。 === 社家 === 神職は、田谷家が代々世襲する。 ==境内社== * 稲荷神社 * 白山神社 - 北生見・南生見に鎮座していた２つの白山神社を遷座した。 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1399 阿志都弥神社]（滋賀県神社庁） {{DEFAULTSORT:あしつみしんしやゆきすきてんまんくう}} [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:郷社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 阿志都弥神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 滋賀県高島市今津町弘川1707-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|24|24.0|N|136|01|32.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 木花開耶姫命
菅原道真公 |社格 = 式内社・旧郷社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造|三間社流造]] |別名 = 桜花大明神・行過天満宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月25日]] |神事= }} '''阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮'''（あしづみじんじゃ・ゆきすぎてんまんぐう）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]今津町弘川にある神社である。[[式内社]]の[[論社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 ==祭神== *阿志都弥神社 ** [[木花開耶姫命]] *行過天満宮 ** [[菅原道真公]] ==神紋== *山桜 ==歴史== 創祀年代不詳であるが、社伝によると、社殿を持たなかった時代、この地にあった山桜の木に木花開耶姫命を[[勧請]]し、「桜花大明神」と称した。[[長徳]]4年（[[998年]]）に[[菅原輔正]]が菅原道真公を勧請し、行過天満天神を併せ祀ったと伝えられている。阿志都弥神社は、式内社論社である。善積郷の惣社である。旧郷社。 ==祭祀== === 神事 === 例祭は4月25日。 === 社家 === 神職は、田谷家が代々世襲する。 ==境内社== * 稲荷神社 * 白山神社 - 北生見・南生見に鎮座していた２つの白山神社を遷座した。 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1399 阿志都弥神社]（滋賀県神社庁） {{DEFAULTSORT:あしつみしんしやゆきすきてんまんくう}} [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:天満宮]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 阿志都弥神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 滋賀県高島市今津町弘川1707-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|24|24.0|N|136|01|32.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 木花開耶姫命
菅原道真公 |社格 = 式内社・旧郷社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造|三間社流造]] |別名 = 桜花大明神・行過天満宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月25日]] |神事= }} '''阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮'''（あしづみじんじゃ・ゆきすぎてんまんぐう）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]今津町弘川にある神社である。[[式内社]]の[[論社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 ==祭神== *阿志都弥神社 ** [[木花開耶姫命]] *行過天満宮 ** [[菅原道真公]] ==神紋== *山桜 ==歴史== 創祀年代不詳であるが、社伝によると、社殿を持たなかった時代、この地にあった山桜の木に木花開耶姫命を[[勧請]]し、「桜花大明神」と称した。[[長徳]]4年（[[998年]]）に[[菅原輔正]]が菅原道真公を勧請し、行過天満天神を併せ祀ったと伝えられている。阿志都弥神社は、式内社論社である。善積郷の惣社である。旧郷社。 ==祭祀== === 神事 === 例祭は4月25日。 === 社家 === 神職は、田谷家が代々世襲する。 ==境内社== * 稲荷神社 * 白山神社 - 北生見・南生見に鎮座していた２つの白山神社を遷座した。 == 外部リンク == {{DEFAULTSORT:あしつみしんしやゆきすきてんまんくう}} [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:天満宮]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: 外部リンクを追加の上、記事を加筆。滋賀県神社庁の当該社の説明はリンク不能のため外部リンク節にトップページを記載。

{{出典の明記|date=2016年7月14日 (木) 08:49 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 阿志都弥神社 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=高島市}} |所在地 = 滋賀県高島市今津町弘川1707-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|24|24.0|N|136|01|32.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 木花開耶姫命
菅原道真公 |社格 = 式内社・旧郷社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造|三間社流造]] |別名 = 桜花大明神・行過天満宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月25日]] |神事= }} '''阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮'''（あしづみじんじゃ・ゆきすぎてんまんぐう）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]今津町弘川にある神社である。[[式内社]]の[[論社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 ==祭神== * 阿志都弥神社 ** [[木花開耶姫命]] * 行過天満宮 ** [[菅原道真公]] ==神紋== * 山桜 梅鉢 ==歴史== 創祀年代不詳であるが、社伝によると、社殿を持たなかった時代、この地にあった山桜の木に木花開耶姫命を[[勧請]]し、「桜花大明神」と称した。[[長徳]]4年（[[998年]]）に[[菅原輔正]]が菅原道真公を勧請し、行過天満天神を併せ祀ったと伝えられている。阿志都弥神社は、式内社論社である。善積郷の惣社である。旧郷社。 ==祭祀== === 神事 === * [[例祭]]は4月25日祭礼日は神事(祭儀)のみ挙行。。 * [[御輿]]が町内を練り歩く「弘川祭」は4月29日（[[昭和の日]]）に実施される。 === 社家 === 神職は、田谷家が代々世襲する。 ==境内社== * 稲荷神社 * 白山神社 - 北生見・南生見に鎮座していた２つの白山神社を遷座した。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ 滋賀県神社庁] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html 阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮（びわ湖高島観光協会）] {{節stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:あしつみしんしやゆきすきてんまんくう}} [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:天満宮]]

{{出典の明記|date=2016年7月14日 (木) 08:49 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 阿志都弥神社 |画像 = [[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = 滋賀県高島市今津町弘川1707-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|24|24.0|N|136|01|32.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 木花開耶姫命
菅原道真公 |社格 = 式内社・旧郷社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造|三間社流造]] |別名 = 桜花大明神・行過天満宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月25日]] |神事= }} '''阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮'''（あしづみじんじゃ・ゆきすぎてんまんぐう）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]今津町弘川にある神社である。[[式内社]]の[[論社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 ==祭神== * 阿志都弥神社 ** [[木花開耶姫命]] * 行過天満宮 ** [[菅原道真公]] ==神紋== * 山桜 梅鉢 ==歴史== 創祀年代不詳であるが、社伝によると、社殿を持たなかった時代、この地にあった山桜の木に木花開耶姫命を[[勧請]]し、「桜花大明神」と称した。[[長徳]]4年（[[998年]]）に[[菅原輔正]]が菅原道真公を勧請し、行過天満天神を併せ祀ったと伝えられている。阿志都弥神社は、式内社論社である。善積郷の惣社である。旧郷社。 ==祭祀== === 神事 === * [[例祭]]は4月25日祭礼日は神事(祭儀)のみ挙行。。 * [[御輿]]が町内を練り歩く「弘川祭」は4月29日（[[昭和の日]]）に実施される。 === 社家 === 神職は、田谷家が代々世襲する。 ==境内社== * 稲荷神社 * 白山神社 - 北生見・南生見に鎮座していた２つの白山神社を遷座した。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ 滋賀県神社庁] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html 阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮（びわ湖高島観光協会）] {{節stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:あしつみしんしやゆきすきてんまんくう}} [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:天満宮]]

{{出典の明記|date=2016年7月14日 (木) 08:49 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称 = 阿志都弥神社 |画像 = [[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = 滋賀県高島市今津町弘川1707-1 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|24|24.0|N|136|01|32.8|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 木花開耶姫命
菅原道真公 |社格 = 式内社・旧郷社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = [[流造|三間社流造]] |別名 = 桜花大明神・行過天満宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月25日]] |神事= }} '''阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮'''（あしづみじんじゃ・ゆきすぎてんまんぐう）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]今津町弘川にある神社である。[[式内社]]の[[論社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 ==祭神== * 阿志都弥神社 ** [[木花開耶姫命]] * 行過天満宮 ** [[菅原道真公]] ==神紋== * 山桜 梅鉢 ==歴史== 創祀年代不詳であるが、社伝によると、社殿を持たなかった時代、この地にあった山桜の木に木花開耶姫命を[[勧請]]し、「桜花大明神」と称した。[[長徳]]4年（[[998年]]）に[[菅原輔正]]が菅原道真公を勧請し、行過天満天神を併せ祀ったと伝えられている。阿志都弥神社は、式内社論社である。善積郷の惣社である。旧郷社。 今津町弘川の地は和名抄の善積郷に含まれると考えられている地である。 この「善積」は本来「葦積」であつて「アシツミ」の社名と同じであつた。 が、それは平安時代以降の意味であって、原初的日本語として考えれば、アシヅミは安曇族のアヅミと同族語である。という説がある。 京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者 吉田金彦 出版社 京都新聞社 発売日 1987 ==祭祀== === 神事 === * [[例祭]]は4月25日祭礼日は神事(祭儀)のみ挙行。。 * [[御輿]]が町内を練り歩く「弘川祭」は4月29日（[[昭和の日]]）に実施される。 === 社家 === 神職は、田谷家が代々世襲する。 ==境内社== * 稲荷神社 * 白山神社 - 北生見・南生見に鎮座していた２つの白山神社を遷座した。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ 滋賀県神社庁] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html 阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮（びわ湖高島観光協会）] {{節stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:あしつみしんしやゆきすきてんまんくう}} [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:天満宮]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称='''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) |画像=[[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] |所在地={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} Imazu-chō Hirokawa 1707-1 |位置={{ill|Coordinates (geographic)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} 35°24′24.0″N 136°01′32.8″E |祭神={{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |社格={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |例祭=[[April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Hirokawa area of {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} (Imazu-chō), in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|lt=old shrine ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == * **Ashizumi Jinja** : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} * **Yukisugi Tenmangū** : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The **annual festival** (例祭) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The **Hirokawa Festival** takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Inari Jinja** * **Hakusan Jinja** — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]]

{{神社 |名称='''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) |画像=[[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] |所在地={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} Imazu-chō Hirokawa 1707-1 |位置={{ill|Coordinates (geographic)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} 35°24′24.0″N 136°01′32.8″E |祭神={{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}

{{神社 |名称='''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) |画像=[[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] |所在地={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} Imazu-chō Hirokawa 1707-1 |位置={{ill|Coordinates (geographic)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} 35°24′24.0″N 136°01′32.8″E |祭神={{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |社格={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |例祭=[[April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Hirokawa area of {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} (Imazu-chō), in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|lt=old shrine ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == * **Ashizumi Jinja** : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} * **Yukisugi Tenmangū** : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The **annual festival** (例祭) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The **Hirokawa Festival** takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Inari Jinja** * **Hakusan Jinja** — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:Unsorted link categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:Unsorted link categories]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]]

It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The **annual festival** (例祭) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The **Hirokawa Festival** takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Inari Jinja** * **Hakusan Jinja** — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]]

Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The **annual festival** (例祭) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The **Hirokawa Festival** takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Inari Jinja** * **Hakusan Jinja** — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]][[category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:images]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]]

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |社格={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |例祭=[[April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Hirokawa area of {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} (Imazu-chō), in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|lt=old shrine ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == * **Ashizumi Jinja** : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} * **Yukisugi Tenmangū** : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The **annual festival** (例祭) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The **Hirokawa Festival** takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Inari Jinja** * **Hakusan Jinja** — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] revision.text:
{{神社 |名称='''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) |画像=[[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] |所在地={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} Imazu-chō Hirokawa 1707-1 |位置={{ill|coordinates (geographic)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} 35°24′24.0″N 136°01′32.8″E |祭神={{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |社格={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |例祭=[[April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Hirokawa area of {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} (Imazu-chō), in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|lt=old shrine ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == * **Ashizumi Jinja** : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} * **Yukisugi Tenmangū** : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The **annual festival** (例祭) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The **Hirokawa Festival** takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Inari Jinja** * **Hakusan Jinja** — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] attribution]][[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:nnn]] 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The **annual festival** (例祭) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The **Hirokawa Festival** takes place on hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Inari Jinja** * **Hakusan Jinja** — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The **annual festival** (例祭) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The **Hirokawa Festival** takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Inari Jinja** * **Hakusan Jinja** — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]]

{{神社 |name='''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) |image=[[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] |所在地={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} Imazu-chō Hirokawa 1707-1 |位置={{ill|coordinates (geographic)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} 35°24′24.0″N 136°01′32.8″E |deity={{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} |社格={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Hirokawa area of {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} (Imazu-chō), in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|lt=old shrine ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == * **Ashizumi Jinja** : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} * **Yukisugi Tenmangū** : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The **annual festival** (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The **Hirokawa Festival** takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Inari Jinja** * **Hakusan Jinja** — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]]

Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The **annual festival** (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The **Hirokawa Festival** takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Inari Jinja** * **Hakusan Jinja** — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū-qq]] [[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū-qq]] [[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:images]] [[Category:nnn]] [[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]]

{{神社 |name='''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) |image=[[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} Imazu-chō Hirokawa 1707-1 |位置={{ill|coordinates (geographic)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} 35°24′24.0″N 136°01′32.8″E |deity={{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Hirokawa area of {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} (Imazu-chō), in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|lt=old shrine ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == * **Ashizumi Jinja** : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} * **Yukisugi Tenmangū** : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The **annual festival** (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The **Hirokawa Festival** takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * **Inari Jinja** * **Hakusan Jinja** — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. {{神社 |name='''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) |image=[[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} Imazu-chō Hirokawa 1707-1 |位置={{ill|coordinates (geographic)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} 35°24′24.0″N 136°01′32.8″E |deity={{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Hirokawa area of {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} (Imazu-chō), in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|lt=old shrine ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. {{神社 |name='''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) |image=[[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} Imazu-chō Hirokawa 1707-1 |位置={{ill|coordinates (geographic)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} 35°24′24.0″N 136°01′32.8″E |deity={{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Hirokawa area of {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} (Imazu-chō), in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|lt=old shrine ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation {{神社 |name='''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) |image=[[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} Imazu-chō Hirokawa 1707-1 |位置={{ill|coordinates (geographic)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} 35°24′24.0″N 136°01′32.8″E |deity={{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Hirokawa area of {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} (Imazu-chō), in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|lt=old shrine ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga revision.text:
{{神社 |name='''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) |image=[[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} Imazu-chō Hirokawa 1707-1 |位置={{ill|coordinates (geographic)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} 35°24′24.0″N 136°01′32.8″E |deity={{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Hirokawa area of {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} (Imazu-chō), in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|lt=old shrine ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:deprecated qq start]][[Category:deprecated qq end]] [[Category:deprecated qq start]][[Category:deprecated qq end]] {{神社 |name='''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) |image=[[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} Imazu-chō Hirokawa 1707-1 |位置={{ill|coordinates (geographic)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} 35°24′24.0″N 136°01′32.8″E |deity={{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Hirokawa area of {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} (Imazu-chō), in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|lt=old shrine ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga {{神社 |name='''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) |image=[[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} Imazu-chō Hirokawa 1707-1 |位置={{ill|coordinates (geographic)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} 35°24′24.0″N 136°01′32.8″E |deity={{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Hirokawa area of {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} (Imazu-chō), in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|lt=old shrine ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Hirokawa area of {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} (Imazu-chō), in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|lt=old shrine ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Hirokawa area of {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} (Imazu-chō), in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|lt=old shrine ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[en:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{神社 |name='''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) |image=[[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location={{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} Imazu-chō Hirokawa 1707-1 |位置={{ill|coordinates (geographic)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒}} 35°24′24.0″N 136°01′32.8″E |deity={{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in the Hirokawa area of {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|ja|高島市}} (Imazu-chō), in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|lt=old shrine ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[en:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[Category:11]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[Category:New]]

According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[en:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]]

According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|12=draft}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|12=draft}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日|12=draft}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|12=draft}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[en:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]]

Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|ja|昭和の日|12=draft|12=draft}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|12=draft|12=draft}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:March 25th orphaned pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[en:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]]

It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft|12=draft}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|lt=old shrine ranks|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|12=draft|12=draft}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|12=draft|12=draft}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū.
