Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Tenman-gū]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Jawiki redirect categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][Category:qq]] [[Category:undid 1 v3 ill-fix edit(s)]]

{{神社 |name='''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) |image=[[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] Imazu-chō Hirokawa 1707-1 |位置={{ill|coordinates (geographic)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/coordinates (geographic)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 35°24′24.0″N 136°01′32.8″E |deity={{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|qq=}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Jawiki redirect categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][Category:qq]] [[Category:undid 1 v3 ill-fix edit(s)]]

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|qq=}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Jawiki redirect categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][Category:qq]]

Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Jawiki redirect categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][Category:qq]]

{{神社 |name='''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) |image=[[File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg|270px]] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] Imazu-chō Hirokawa 1707-1 |位置={{ill|coordinates (geographic)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/coordinates (geographic)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 35°24′24.0″N 136°01′32.8″E |deity={{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|qq=}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Jawiki redirect categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|qq=}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Jawiki redirect categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Jawiki redirect categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Takashima|qid=Q845794}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.406667|N|136.025778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|qq=}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Jawiki redirect categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Takashima|qid=Q845794}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.406667|N|136.025778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|qq=|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Jawiki redirect categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|qq=|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Jawiki redirect categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Takashima|qid=Q845794}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.406667|N|136.025778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|qq=|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Jawiki redirect categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Takashima|qid=Q845794}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.406667|N|136.025778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|qq=|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Jawiki redirect categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Takashima|qid=Q845794}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.406667|N|136.025778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|qq=|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Jawiki redirect categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Takashima|qid=Q845794}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.406667|N|136.025778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|qq=|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Jawiki redirect categories]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takashima, Shiga]] [[sn:Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū]]
