{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Takashima|WD=Q845794}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.406667|N|136.025778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11657432]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11657432]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name='''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) |image=File:Ashidumi-Shrine-Yukisugi-Tenmangu.jpg | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|location=[[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] Imazu-chō Hirokawa 1707-1 |位置={{ill|coordinates (geographic)|ja|ウィキ座標2段度分秒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/coordinates (geographic)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 35°24′24.0″N 136°01′32.8″E |deity={{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Takashima|WD=Q845794}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.406667|N|136.025778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Takashima|WD=Q845794}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.406667|N|136.025778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Takashima|WD=Q845794}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.406667|N|136.025778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Takashima|WD=Q845794}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.406667|N|136.025778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Takashima|WD=Q845794}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.406667|N|136.025778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū (Q11657432)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenmangū|lt=Ashizumi Shrine / Yukisugi Tenmangū|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|WD=Q11657432}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || — || — || {{ill|Ashitsumino Shrine|lt=Ashitsumino Shrine|WD=Q135039871}}; {{ill|Ashitsumino Shrine|lt=Ashitsumino Shrine|WD=Q135039871}} || {{coord|35.406569|136.026072|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181893 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] {{wikidata link|Q11657432}}

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Takashima|WD=Q845794}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.406667|N|136.025778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū (Q11657432)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū|lt=Ashizumi Shrine / Yukisugi Tenmangū|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|WD=Q11657432}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || — || — || {{ill|Ashitsumino Shrine|lt=Ashitsumino Shrine|WD=Q135039871}}; {{ill|Ashitsumino Shrine|lt=Ashitsumino Shrine|WD=Q135039871}} || {{coord|35.406569|136.026072|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181893 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] {{wikidata link|Q11657432}}

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Takashima|WD=Q845794}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.406667|N|136.025778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū|lt=Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|WD=Q11657432}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || — || — || {{ill|Ashitsumino Shrine|lt=Ashitsumino Shrine|WD=Q135039871}}; {{ill|Ashitsumino Shrine|lt=Ashitsumino Shrine|WD=Q135039871}} || {{coord|35.406569|136.026072|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181893 DB] |} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] {{wikidata link|Q11657432}}

It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū (Q11657432)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū|lt=Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|WD=Q11657432}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || — || — || {{ill|Ashitsumino Shrine|lt=Ashitsumino Shrine|WD=Q135039871}}; {{ill|Ashitsumino Shrine|lt=Ashitsumino Shrine|WD=Q135039871}} || {{coord|35.406569|136.026072|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181893 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11657432}} [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]]

It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū (Q11657432)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū|lt=Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|WD=Q11657432}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || — || — || {{ill|Ashitsumino Shrine|lt=Ashitsumino Shrine|WD=Q135039871}}; {{ill|Ashitsumino Shrine|lt=Ashitsumino Shrine|WD=Q135039871}} || {{coord|35.406569|136.026072|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181893 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11657432}} [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]]==Notes==

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival=[[:en:April 25|April 25]] |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Takashima|WD=Q845794}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.406667|N|136.025778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in the Hirokawa area of [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima, Shiga]] (Imazu-chō), in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|old shrine Ranks]], it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on [[:en:April 25|April 25]]Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ([[:en:Shōwa Day|Shōwa Day]]), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū (Q11657432)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū|lt=Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|WD=Q11657432}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || — || — || {{ill|Ashitsumino Shrine|lt=Ashitsumino Shrine|WD=Q135039871}}; {{ill|Ashitsumino Shrine|lt=Ashitsumino Shrine|WD=Q135039871}} || {{coord|35.406569|136.026072|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181893 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11657432}} [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]]==Notes==

{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} · Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿=[[Flowing style shrine|三間社流造]] |別名=Sakurabana Daimyōjin / Yukisugi Tenmangū |札所等= |festival={{ill|April 25|en|April 25|WD=Q2531|lt=April 25}} |神事= |prefecture={{ill|Takashima|WD=Q845794}} |coordinates={{Coord|35.406667|N|136.025778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style={{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Ashizumi Jinja / Yukisugi Tenman-gū''' (阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the Hirokawa area of {{ill|Takashima, Shiga|en|Takashima, Shiga|WD=Q845794|lt=Takashima, Shiga}} (Imazu-chō), in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine Ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on {{ill|April 25|en|April 25|WD=Q2531|lt=April 25}}Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|en|Shōwa Day|WD=Q1361434|lt=Shōwa Day}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū (Q11657432)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū|lt=Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|WD=Q11657432}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || — || — || {{ill|Ashitsumino Shrine|lt=Ashitsumino Shrine|WD=Q135039871}}; {{ill|Ashitsumino Shrine|lt=Ashitsumino Shrine|WD=Q135039871}} || {{coord|35.406569|136.026072|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181893 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11657432}} [[da:d:Q11657432]] [[ja:阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]]==Notes==

It is a candidate (ronsha) for the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listing, and under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=old shrine Ranks}}, it was classified as a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Enshrined Deities == * '''Ashizumi Jinja''' : {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} * '''Yukisugi Tenmangū''' : {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} == Crest (Kamon) == * Yamazakura (mountain cherry) * Umebachi (stylized plum blossom) == History == The exact year of founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, before a dedicated sanctuary existed, {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花開耶姫命|1=Konohanasakuya-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}} was enshrined on a local wild cherry tree, hence the name “Sakurabana Daimyōjin.” In the 4th year of {{ill|Chōtoku (era)|ja|長徳 (日本)|lt=Chōtoku|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōtoku (era)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (998), {{ill|Sugawara no Sukemasa|ja|菅原輔正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sukemasa|1=Sugawara no Sukemasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17218735}} is said to have invited the spirit of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} to this site, creating the dual dedication to Yukisugi Tenmangū. Ashizumi Jinja is considered a ronsha (candidate shrine) for one of the ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. It served as the sōsha (general shrine) of the Zenzumigō region and later became a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. Imazu-chō Hirokawa is regarded as within the area of the “Zenzumi-gō” mentioned in “Wamyō Ruijushō.” The name “Zenzumi” may originally have been “Ashizumi” (written as 葦積), matching the pronunciation “Ashi-tsumi” of the shrine’s name—though from a more archaic linguistic perspective, “Ashizumi” may have roots in “Azumi” (安曇), suggesting a link with the Azumi clan.京都滋賀古代地名を歩く 著者: 吉田金彦 出版社: 京都新聞社 発売日: 1987 == Festivals == === Rituals === * The '''annual festival''' (festival) is held on {{ill|April 25|en|April 25|WD=Q2531|lt=April 25}}Only religious rites (saigi) are performed on this day.. * The '''Hirokawa Festival''' takes place on April 29 ({{ill|Shōwa Day|en|Shōwa Day|WD=Q1361434|lt=Shōwa Day}}), featuring a parade where a {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|御輿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神輿}} is carried through the local community. === Shrine Priest Family === The Taya family has historically held the hereditary priesthood for this shrine. == Subsidiary Shrines == * '''Inari Jinja''' * '''Hakusan Jinja''' — Merged from two separate Hakusan shrines formerly in Kita-Umimi and Minami-Umimi. == Notes == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines] * [http://www.takashima-kanko.jp/spot/imazu/408.html Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū (Takashima Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|阿志都弥神社・行過天満宮|version=82167212|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ashizumi Shrine & Yukisugi Tenmangū (Q11657432)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]].
